name: Architecture View
description: VS Code WebviewView sidebar panel ("Architecture") that renders the architecture wiki as a force-directed component graph (nodes view) plus search, a list view, and a card detail/editor. Hosts the shared @code-workbench/ui ArchPanel React component and answers its list/upsert/remove/openFile RPC calls against the repo's .code-workbench/.arch/<slug>.json cards — the SAME store the bundled arch MCP server (arch-server.mjs) reads and writes, so cards Claude authors via seed-arch/arch_upsert and cards edited in the panel are one and the same files. Ported from the removed Electron app's ArchWikiPane + ArchGraph.
guidelines: Arch cards are JSON files under .code-workbench/.arch/<slug>.json — the same store the arch MCP server (arch-server.mjs) uses; never invent a parallel store. archHost.ts reads/writes them with an atomic tmp+rename, a per-file write lock, and a safe-slug + path-traversal guard, so a panel edit and a concurrent MCP write can't corrupt a card.

The panel UI is the shared ui ArchPanel (graph + search + list + editor) rendered via a React webview; do not hand-write panel markup. Pass hideHeaderTitle — the WebviewView chrome already shows the 'Architecture' pane name.

archHost.ts declares its own ArchCard interface (structurally identical to the ui ArchCard). Host src/ code must NOT import the type from the JSX @code-workbench/ui barrel: the extension's src tsconfig has no jsx set and excludes webview/, so importing the barrel pulls .tsx into a non-jsx program and fails the build. Mirror this exactly the way DeadCodeItem is duplicated between mcp-core and ui.

Push live updates with a vscode.FileSystemWatcher on the arch dir (RelativePattern '*.json') that posts an 'arch-changed' event, so cards written by seed-arch / the arch MCP appear without a manual refresh. Dispose the watcher on view dispose and re-point it on workspace-folder change via onRepoChanged().

The webview entry must stay in lockstep across three places: esbuild.mjs entryPoints ('webview/arch'), the WebviewEntry union in reactWebview.ts ('arch'), and ArchViewProvider.viewId / the package.json view id ('codeWorkbench.arch') + view/title refresh command.

Open card 'Key Files' in the editor via the openFile RPC handler (vscode.workspace.openTextDocument); the file paths are repo-relative so the webview joins them onto repoPath, exactly as the scan panels do.
anti_patterns: Do not implement arch-card CRUD anywhere but archHost.ts, and keep its on-disk format byte-compatible with arch-server.mjs (same fields, atomic write) so the panel and the MCP server never clobber each other's files.
Do not import @code-workbench/ui (a JSX package) from extension host src/ code — only the webview/*.tsx entries may; host code uses the local ArchCard interface in archHost.ts.
Do not rebuild the arch graph/search/editor as vanilla-JS webview HTML — render the shared ui ArchPanel/ArchGraph.
Do not leak the FileSystemWatcher: dispose it on view dispose and before re-pointing it, or repo switches accumulate watchers.
decisions: The Architecture panel was ported from the removed Electron app's ArchWikiPane + ArchGraph into the shared ui package so it renders identically; the Electron useArchWiki/window.api hook was replaced by an injected ArchApi adapter wired over the webview postMessage RPC bridge (webviewRpc.ts / bridge.ts).
Live refresh uses a VS Code FileSystemWatcher instead of the Electron app's chokidar + coalesce watcher; event debouncing is delegated to VS Code's watcher.
ArchGraph keeps its deterministic force-directed layout (seeded by slug hash) and reads the clay design tokens with literal fallbacks, so it needs no host data beyond the cards array.
Card 'Key Files' are clickable and open in the editor via the host openFile RPC — a new affordance over the Electron original.
